spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates exactly the spec's value proposition: `rawgamepadinputchange` firing on each device transition vs `navigator.getGamepads()` polled in rAF. Both code paths run in parallel and report interval averages.

interactive.rationale: Real API surface — listens for `gamepadconnected`, attaches the event listener with feature detection, and updates two big-number readouts on a 250ms interval.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises two panels showing interval between input events, polled vs event-driven; the page delivers exactly that with the lower-is-better framing.

edge_cases.rationale: Feature-detection fallback (`if 'onrawgamepadinputchange' in window`) is good. But: no keyboard simulation for visitors without a gamepad (the sibling Axes Explorer has one), and no handling for the gamepad-not-yet-polled state where Chrome requires button activity before populating `getGamepads()`.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only, including `--accent-amber` and `--accent-blue` for the poll vs event panel borders. No button-background overrides needed. No static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add keyboard simulation for visitors without a controller
openQuestions.detail: The sibling Axes Explorer and Combo Detector concepts include a keyboard sim so the demo is testable without hardware. This page is currently blank until a gamepad is plugged in — most viewers will see `no controller detected` and bounce. Add a small `simulate inputs` mode that dispatches synthetic events at known frequencies (60Hz, 120Hz, 240Hz) so the latency contrast is visible even on a laptop with no controller.

openQuestions.title: Show sub-frame events the polling path misses
openQuestions.detail: The feature's actual win is catching the inputs that arrive between frames — not just the average interval. Add a counter `events seen between two rAF ticks` so the visitor can read out `polled saw 1 transition this frame, event-driven saw 4`. That is the concrete number that justifies the feature.

openQuestions.title: Add a peak / max-interval readout
openQuestions.detail: Average interval can mask jank — a 100ms stall while average stays at 16ms tells a different story. Add max-interval-since-start to both panels.

openQuestions.title: Note the OT flag requirement explicitly on the page
openQuestions.detail: The feature is in an origin trial. The page falls back gracefully when `onrawgamepadinputchange` is missing but doesn't tell the user how to enable it (chrome://flags or the OT token). A small banner with the chrome://flags entry name would close that loop.
